summary: ViMMS is a flexible and modular framework designed to simulate fragmentation strategies in tandem mass spectrometry-based metabolomics.

ViMMS allows you to simulate fragmentation strategies, generate virtual chemicals through various methods, and evaluate the performance of different strategies or controllers in either single or multi-sample settings.
Known chemicals in ViMMS are represented by their chemical formulae and can be sampled from databases such as HMDB.
DatabaseFormulaSampler samples formulas from a provided database. PickEverythingFormulaSampler samples all formulas from a database.
UniformMZFormulaSampler samples formulas uniformly in a defined m/z range.
EvenMZFormulaSampler creates evenly spaced m/z values, primarily for test cases.
MZMLFormulaSampler samples m/z values from a histogram of m/z derived from a user-supplied mzML file.
ChemicalMixtureCreator class is used to produce chemical datasets for simulation.
Environment class orchestrates interaction between the mass spectrometer and the controller in ViMMS.
A simulation in ViMMS involves Chemicals, Mass Spectrometer, Controller, and Environment components.
Running the environment in ViMMS produces a list of scans that can be written to mzML.
FullScanController in ViMMS is useful for creating full scan datasets through MS1 only acquisition.
Environment class provides write_mzML to export the generated scans.
ChemicalMixtureFromMZML converts existing mzML files into chemical lists using ROI extraction.
data_generation.py generates example chemical mixtures and mzML files useful for testing pipelines or benchmarking fragmenters.
